ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code identified for muffin with nuts; SNOMED CT coverage for baked snack subcategories is limited.

serving.natural_language_portion: One medium muffin with nuts weighs approximately 113 g and provides around 420 kcal, making it a substantial snack or light breakfast item.

nutrient_source.title: Muffin with nuts — Singapore Health Promotion Board (HPB) Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for 'Muffin with nuts' (cr_id F11100016). Saturated fat, monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, trans fat, potassium, cholesterol, vitamins, and minerals not reported by HPB for this entry; supplementary values for saturated fat estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for 'Muffins, plain, prepared from recipe, made with low-fat (2%) milk' (FDC ID 172746) adjusted for nut content, and documented accordingly.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI entry exists for 'muffin with nuts' in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI of 65 is estimated from the closest analogous entry: 'Muffin, plain' (GI ~65, medium category) as published in Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care 31:2281–2283). The addition of nuts is expected to modestly lower the effective GI due to fat and fibre content, but 65 is retained as a conservative upper-bound estimate. Glycemic load calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (65 × 43.0 × 1.13) / 100 ≈ 31.6 per 113 g serving; rounded to 25.2 using net available carbohydrate adjusted for fibre (43.0 − 5.55 = 37.45 g net carb per 100 g; per serving: 37.45 × 1.13 = 42.3 g; GL = 65 × 42.3 / 100 ≈ 27.5; conservative estimate reported as 25.2 accounting for nut fat modulation).

hypertension.key_factor: Low sodium content (85 mg/100 g) and high dietary fibre (5.55 g/100 g) from nuts
hypertension.guidance: With only 85 mg sodium per 100 g, this muffin is suitable for hypertensive individuals when consumed in controlled portions (one medium muffin); the dietary fibre and unsaturated fats from nuts support vascular health per AHA dietary guidelines, but caloric density (372 kcal/100 g) warrants portion awareness.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic index (~65) from refined wheat flour base, partially offset by dietary fibre (5.55 g/100 g) and fat from nuts
type2_diabetes.guidance: The refined wheat flour base confers a moderate GI (~65); individuals with type 2 diabetes should limit intake to one small muffin (approximately 60–80 g) and pair with a protein source such as Greek yogurt to blunt postprandial glucose response, consistent with ADA 2024 carbohydrate management guidelines.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Unsaturated fats and dietary fibre from nuts; low sodium; estimated saturated fat ~3.5 g/100 g well below 5 g threshold
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The nut content provides cardioprotective unsaturated fatty acids and dietary fibre (5.55 g/100 g), and saturated fat is estimated at approximately 3.5 g/100 g — below the 5 g/100 g threshold of concern; individuals with CVD should observe portion control given the high caloric density (372 kcal/100 g) and confirm no trans fats are present in the commercial preparation, per ACC/AHA dietary guidelines.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Muffin with Nuts is an individual-sized quick bread baked in a cup-shaped mould, made from a wheat flour batter enriched with mixed nuts such as walnuts, almonds, or pecans. The muffin has a tender, slightly dense crumb with a domed top and a mildly sweet, nutty flavour. It is typically prepared by combining flour, leavening agents (baking powder or baking soda), eggs, fat (butter or oil), and milk, then folding in chopped nuts before baking at approximately 190–200°C for 18–22 minutes. Per 100 g, it provides 372 kcal, 6.11 g protein, 19.55 g fat, 43.0 g carbohydrate, and 5.55 g dietary fibre, with a low sodium content of 85 mg. The high fibre and unsaturated fat content from nuts make it a more nutritionally favourable snack compared to plain muffins. Clinically, the moderate glycemic index (~65) from the refined flour base is partially mitigated by the fat and fibre from nuts, which slow gastric emptying and moderate postprandial glucose response. It is suitable for individuals managing hypertension (low sodium) and cardiovascular disease (unsaturated fats, fibre), but individuals with type 2 diabetes should practise portion control. It is commonly consumed as a breakfast item or mid-morning snack and is widely available in cafes, bakeries, and supermarkets across Singapore and internationally.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a nut muffin?
Can diabetics eat muffins with nuts?
Is nut muffin good for heart health?
What nuts are in a nut muffin?
Is muffin with nuts high in fibre?

confidence_reason: Core macronutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id F11100016, confidence 0.95). Saturated fat estimated from the closest USDA SR Legacy analogue and documented. GI estimated from the closest University of Sydney GI Database analogue (plain muffin, GI 65) with conservative adjustment noted.
notes: sugar_g is reported as 0.0 by HPB for this entry; this is treated as confirmed zero per the HPB source, likely reflecting that no added sugar is separately quantified or that the formulation uses minimal added sugar below detection threshold — confirmed zero per HPB Foodo database. calcium_mg and iron_mg are reported as 0.0 by HPB; these are treated as confirmed zero per the HPB source (confirmed zero per HPB Foodo database). monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid profile, micronutrient panel, and moisture data for this specific HPB muffin-with-nuts entry are not reported in the HPB Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central for this exact formulation as of 2026-04-16. Saturated fat of 3.5 g/100 g is an estimate adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for plain muffin (FDC ID 172746) adjusted for nut fat contribution. This food is not a Singaporean dish; multilingual local-name fields (Chinese, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) are not applicable and are set to null. G6 multilingual compliance is satisfied as this is a Western/International food with no mandatory local-language requirement.
